question: We want to construct a covariant modified gravity theory that reproduces the successes of MOND at galactic scales while remaining compatible with ALL other observational scales: (1) SOLAR SYSTEM: recover GR to PPN precision (gamma, beta ~1), no fifth-force anomalies, correct perihelion precession. (2) GALAXIES: reproduce the Radial Acceleration Relation (RAR/MDAR), baryonic Tully-Fisher relation (v^4 ~ G M a0), flat rotation curves, with a0 ~ 1.2e-10 m/s^2. (3) GALAXY CLUSTERS: explain cluster mass discrepancy without invoking CDM — or if sterile neutrino dark matter is needed, explain why and at what scale. (4) CMB: reproduce the acoustic peak positions and heights, and the matter power spectrum P(k), without CDM — or specify precisely what supplementary matter component is required and what are its properties. (5) LARGE-SCALE STRUCTURE: correct growth rate of structure, BAO scale, Hubble tension. The theory must be relativistically covariant. Candidate frameworks include: TeVeS (Bekenstein 2004), RMOND/AMOND (Skordis-Zlosnik 2021), bimetric MOND (Milgrom 2009), dipolar dark matter (Blanchet), emergent gravity (Verlinde), covariant MOND action theories (Bruneton & Esposito-Farese 2007). Identify the strongest candidate, write its covariant action, derive its field equations, check each scale, and propose what additional ingredient (if any) is needed to pass all tests simultaneously.
title: Covariant Modified Gravity at All Scales: From Solar System to CMB

responses.GR Expert:
# Covariant MOND: A Rigorous Multi-Scale Analysis

## Executive Summary

No single existing framework passes all five tests simultaneously. The **strongest candidate** is the **RMOND/AMOND framework of Skordis & Złośnik (2021)** — hereafter **AeST (Aether-Scalar-Tensor)** theory — which is the first covariant MOND theory consistent with gravitational wave speed $c_{\rm GW} = c$ and capable of fitting CMB+LSS. However, it requires a supplementary component at cluster scales. I will derive everything from scratch, then propose a minimal extension.

---

## Part I: Why Predecessors Fail

### 1.1 TeVeS (Bekenstein 2004)

The action is:

$$S_{\rm TeVeS} = S_{\rm EH} + S_\phi + S_V + S_{\rm matter}[\tilde{g}_{\mu\nu}]$$

with scalar field action:

$$S_\phi = -\frac{1}{2}\int d^4x \sqrt{-g}\left[\mu \phi_{,\mu}\phi^{,\mu} + V(\mu)\right]$$

and vector field $A^\mu$ (unit timelike) used to build the physical metric:

$$\tilde{g}_{\mu\nu} = e^{-2\phi}g_{\mu\nu} - 2\sinh(2\phi)A_\mu A_\nu$$

**Fatal problems:**
- Superluminal propagation of scalar perturbations in certain backgrounds
- After GW170817: $|c_{\rm GW}/c - 1| < 10^{-15}$ kills the original TeVeS vector sector
- CMB acoustic peaks: wrong peak ratios without CDM (Slosar et al. 2005; Dodelson & Liguori 2006)
- Growth factor $D(a)$: insufficient power spectrum amplitude

### 1.2 Emergent Gravity (Verlinde 2016)

The elastic response of de Sitter entropy gives an "apparent dark matter" column density:

$$\Sigma_D = \sqrt{\frac{M_b a_0}{8\pi G}} \cdot \frac{1}{r}$$

This is not a covariant field theory. It lacks:
- A well-defined stress-energy tensor $T^{\mu\nu}$
- Any prediction for dynamical perturbations or gravitational waves
- A Cauchy initial-value formulation

**Verdict: Not a theory — a heuristic.**

### 1.3 Dipolar Dark Matter (Blanchet & Le Tiec 2009)

Introduces a polarization field $P^\mu$ with action:

$$S_{\rm DM} = \int d^4x \sqrt{-g}\left[-\rho_- u^\mu_{-}u_{\mu-} - \rho_+ u^\mu_+ u_{\mu+} + \lambda P^\mu P_\mu\right]$$

The dipole moment $d^\mu$ in a gravitational field $g_\mu$ yields MOND phenomenology when $|g| \ll a_0$. Passes solar system (screening), partially passes galaxies. But the dark matter is still physically present — defeats the purpose — and CMB predictions are not fully worked out.

---

## Part II: The AeST Action in Full

### 2.1 Field Content

| Field | Type | Role |
|-------|------|------|
| $g_{\mu\nu}$ | Metric | Gravitational sector |
| $\phi$ | Scalar | MOND interpolating function |
| $A^\mu$ | Unit vector ($A^\mu A_\mu = -1$) | Preferred frame / aether |

### 2.2 The Full Covariant Action

$$\boxed{S = \frac{1}{16\pi G}\int d^4x\,\sqrt{-g}\left[R - 2\Lambda - K_B\mathcal{F}(Q) - \lambda(A^\mu A_\mu + 1)\right] + S_{\rm matter}[\Psi, g_{\mu\nu}]}$$

where the **kinetic MOND term** is:

$$Q \equiv K_B^{-1}\left[-A^\mu A^\nu \nabla_\mu\phi\nabla_\nu\phi + \mathcal{H}^{\mu\nu}\nabla_\mu\phi\nabla_\nu\phi\right]$$

More precisely, define:

$$Q = -\ell^2 A^\mu\nabla_\mu\phi A^\nu\nabla_\nu\phi + \ell^2 h^{\mu\nu}\nabla_\mu\phi\nabla_\nu\phi$$

with $h^{\mu\nu} = g^{\mu\nu} + A^\mu A^\nu$ (spatial projector), and $\ell = (G a_0)^{-1/2}/c^2$ sets the MOND scale.

The **complete Skordis-Złośnik action** is:

$$S = \frac{1}{16\pi G}\int d^4x\sqrt{-g}\Bigg[R - 2K_B\mathcal{F}(Q) - 2\lambda(A^\mu A_\mu + 1)$$
$$- K_A\left(c_1 \nabla_\mu A_\nu \nabla^\mu A^\nu + c_2(\nabla_\mu A^\mu)^2 + c_3 \nabla_\mu A_\nu \nabla^\nu A^\mu\right)\Bigg] + S_m$$

where $c_1, c_2, c_3$ are dimensionless aether coupling constants, $K_A, K_B$ are coupling strengths, and $\mathcal{F}$ is the **free function** encoding MOND interpolation.

### 2.3 Choosing $\mathcal{F}(Q)$

The free function must satisfy:

| Regime | $Q$ value | Required $\mathcal{F}$ behavior | Physical consequence |
|--------|-----------|--------------------------------|---------------------|
| Deep MOND ($a \ll a_0$) | $Q \to 0$ | $\mathcal{F} \sim Q^{3/2}$ | $v^4 \propto GMa_0$ |
| Newtonian ($a \gg a_0$) | $Q \to \infty$ | $\mathcal{F} \sim Q$ | GR recovered |
| Cosmological | $Q \sim 1$ | Smooth interpolation | CMB/LSS |

A canonical choice (Skordis-Złośnik 2021):

$$\mathcal{F}(Q) = Q - Q_0 + \frac{2}{3}Q_0\left(\frac{Q}{Q_0}\right)^{3/2}\Theta(Q_0 - Q) + \cdots$$

or the family parameterized by:

$$\mathcal{F}(Q) = \frac{2Q^{3/2}}{3\sqrt{Q_c}} + Q - Q_c + \mathcal{O}\left(\left(\frac{Q}{Q_c}\right)^2\right)$$

with $Q_c \sim 1$ sets the Newtonian/MOND crossover.

---

## Part III: Field Equations

### 3.1 Metric Equation (Modified Einstein)

Varying $S$ with respect to $g^{\mu\nu}$:

$$G_{\mu\nu} + \Lambda g_{\mu\nu} = 8\pi G\, T^{\rm matter}_{\mu\nu} + T^{\phi}_{\mu\nu} + T^{A}_{\mu\nu} + \lambda A_\mu A_\nu$$

where the scalar stress-energy is:

$$T^{\phi}_{\mu\nu} = K_B\left[\mathcal{F}'(Q)\frac{\partial Q}{\partial g^{\mu\nu}} - \frac{1}{2}g_{\mu\nu}\mathcal{F}(Q)\right]$$

Explicitly:

$$T^{\phi}_{\mu\nu} = K_B\mathcal{F}'(Q)\ell^2 h^{\alpha}_{\ \mu} h^{\beta}_{\ \nu}\nabla_\alpha\phi\nabla_\beta\phi - \frac{K_B}{2}g_{\mu\nu}\mathcal{F}(Q)$$

The aether stress-energy:

$$T^A_{\mu\nu} = K_A\left[c_1\left(\nabla^\alpha A_\mu \nabla_\alpha A_\nu - \nabla_\mu A^\alpha\nabla_\nu A_\alpha\right) + \text{divergence terms}\right] + \lambda A_\mu A_\nu$$

### 3.2 Scalar Field Equation

Varying with respect to $\phi$:

$$\nabla_\mu\left(K_B\mathcal{F}'(Q)\cdot \ell^2 h^{\mu\nu}\nabla_\nu\phi\right) - \nabla_\mu\left(K_B\mathcal{F}'(Q)\cdot \ell^2 A^\mu A^\nu\nabla_\nu\phi\right) = 0$$

$$\Rightarrow \quad \nabla_\mu\left[\mathcal{F}'(Q)\cdot J^\mu\right] = 0, \qquad J^\mu \equiv \ell^2\left(h^{\mu\nu} - 2A^\mu A^\nu\right)\nabla_\nu\phi$$

Wait — more carefully, since $Q$ depends on $A^\mu A^\nu \nabla_\mu\phi\nabla_\nu\phi$ and $h^{\mu\nu}\nabla_\mu\phi\nabla_\nu\phi$:

$$\frac{\partial Q}{\partial(\nabla_\mu\phi)} = 2\ell^2\left(-A^\mu(A^\nu\nabla_\nu\phi) + h^{\mu\nu}\nabla_\nu\phi\right) \equiv 2\ell^2 \hat{J}^\mu$$

So the scalar EOM is:

$$\boxed{\nabla_\mu\left(K_B\mathcal{F}'(Q)\hat{J}^\mu\right) = 0}$$

This is a **nonlinear, covariant scalar equation** that in the quasi-static limit recovers the MOND Poisson equation:

$$\nabla\cdot\left[\mu\!\left(\frac{|\nabla\Phi|}{a_0}\right)\nabla\Phi\right] = 4\pi G\rho_b$$

with $\mu(x) = \mathcal{F}'$ evaluated at the appropriate $Q$.

### 3.3 Vector (Aether) Equation

Varying with respect to $A^\mu$:

$$K_A\mathcal{E}^\mu_{\ \nu} A^\nu = \lambda A^\mu + K_B\mathcal{F}'(Q)\frac{\partial Q}{\partial A^\mu}$$

where $\mathcal{E}^\mu_{\ \nu}$ is the Einstein-aether tensor (see Jacobson 2001). The constraint $A^\mu A_\mu = -1$ is enforced by $\lambda$ as a Lagrange multiplier, determined algebraically by contracting with $A_\mu$:

$$\lambda = K_A A_\mu\mathcal{E}^\mu_{\ \nu}A^\nu - K_B\mathcal{F}'(Q)A_\mu\frac{\partial Q}{\partial A^\mu}$$

---

## Part IV: Scale-by-Scale Analysis

### 4.1 Solar System Tests

**Strategy:** Show that $Q \gg Q_c$ (deep Newtonian regime) near the Sun.

The MOND acceleration scale is $a_0 \approx 1.2\times 10^{-10}$ m/s². At 1 AU from the Sun:

$$g_\odot = \frac{GM_\odot}{r^2}\Bigg|_{1\,\rm AU} \approx 6\times 10^{-3}\,\rm m/s^2 \gg a_0$$

So $Q \gg Q_c$ and $\mathcal{F}(Q) \approx Q$ (linear regime). The scalar decouples as a free field with negligible stress-energy, and the aether reduces to a nearly-constant background unit vector aligned with cosmic time.

The PPN parameters:

$$\gamma_{\rm PPN} - 1 \sim \mathcal{O}\left(\frac{a_0}{g_\odot}\right)^{1/2} \sim \mathcal{O}(10^{-4})$$

Below current Cassini bound $|\gamma - 1| < 2.3\times 10^{-5}$ **only if** $K_A \lesssim 0.01$ (aether couplings small). The perihelion precession receives corrections:

$$\delta\dot{\omega} \sim \dot{\omega}_{\rm GR}\left(1 + \mathcal{O}(K_A c_i)\right)$$

So we need:

$$\boxed{c_1 - c_3 < 10^{-2}, \quad K_A < 10^{-2}}$$

These constraints are compatible with the theory.

**Vainshtein-like screening:** Unlike Galileon/Horndeski theories, AeST has no Vainshtein mechanism per se, but the MOND field is suppressed in high-$g$ environments by the smallness of $\mathcal{F}'(Q) \to 1$ (approach GR). This is the analog of screening.

### 4.2 Galaxy Rotation Curves

In the quasi-static, weak-field limit, with metric $ds^2 = -(1+2\Phi)dt^2 + (1-2\Psi)d\vec{x}^2$, and $A^\mu \approx (1-\Phi, 0, 0, 0)/c$, the scalar EOM reduces to:

$$\nabla\cdot\left[\mu\!\left(\frac{|\nabla\tilde{\Phi}|}{a_0}\right)\nabla\tilde{\Phi}\right] = 4\pi G\rho_b$$

where $\tilde{\Phi}$ is a combination of $\Phi$ and $\phi$.

**Baryonic Tully-Fisher:** In the deep MOND limit $\mu \to |\nabla\tilde\Phi|/a_0$:

$$\nabla\cdot\left[\frac{|\nabla\tilde\Phi|}{a_0}\nabla\tilde\Phi\right] = 4\pi G\rho_b$$

For a point mass with spherical symmetry:

$$\frac{g^2}{a_0} \cdot 4\pi r^2 = 4\pi G M_b \implies g = \sqrt{\frac{GM_b a_0}{r^2}}$$

Setting $g = v^2/r$ for circular orbits:

$$\boxed{v^4 = GM_b a_0}$$

This is **exact** in the deep MOND limit and matches observations over 4 decades in mass (McGaugh et al. 2016). The scatter is $<0.1$ dex, consistent with the baryons-only prediction.

**RAR:** The full radial acceleration relation:

$$g_{\rm obs} = \frac{g_{\rm bar}}{1 - e^{-\sqrt{g_{\rm bar}/a_0}}}$$

is reproduced by choosing:

$$\mu(x) = \frac{x}{1 + x}, \quad x = \frac{|\nabla\Phi|}{a_0}$$

or equivalently $\mathcal{F}'(Q) = Q^{1/2}/(Q^{1/2}+Q_c^{1/2})$. This matches 153 galaxies in the SPARC sample to within observational uncertainty.

responses.Math Expert:
# Covariant Modified Gravity for MOND: A Complete Mathematical Analysis

## Table of Contents
1. Framework Assessment
2. The Strongest Candidate: RMOND (AeST Theory)
3. Complete Covariant Action
4. Field Equations via Variational Principle
5. Scale-by-Scale Analysis
6. Mathematical Consistency Checks
7. Deficiencies and Required Additional Ingredients
8. Proposed Synthesis

---

## 1. Framework Assessment

Let me first systematically eliminate weaker candidates.

### 1.1 TeVeS (Bekenstein 2004) — **Ruled Out**

The action is:
$$S_{\text{TeVeS}} = S_g + S_\phi + S_A + S_m$$

where the scalar sector contains:
$$S_\phi = -\frac{1}{16\pi G}\int d^4x\,\sqrt{-g}\left[\mu \tilde{g}^{\mu\nu}\partial_\mu\phi\partial_\nu\phi + V(\mu)\right]$$

**Fatal flaw:** TeVeS predicts gravitational wave speed $c_{gw} \neq c$ unless fine-tuned. More severely, the gravitational lensing from TeVeS without neutrinos predicts:
$$\Sigma_{\text{lensing}} \neq \Sigma_{\text{dynamical}}$$
in galaxy clusters by factors of $\sim 2$–$4$. Post-GW170817 (requiring $|c_{gw}/c - 1| < 5\times 10^{-16}$), the original TeVeS vector field is constrained to have $c_A = c$, which forces the theory into a degenerate sector. Additionally, TeVeS fails CMB peak heights without a dominant CDM component.

### 1.2 Bimetric MOND (Milgrom 2009) — **Incomplete**

Uses two metrics $g_{\mu\nu}$ and $\hat{g}_{\mu\nu}$ with matter coupling to a third composite metric. The constraint algebra between the two Einstein-Hilbert sectors does not close without additional structure, leading to Boulware-Deser ghost problems unless the Hassan-Rosen conditions are imposed, which breaks the MOND interpolation function freedom.

### 1.3 Dipolar Dark Matter (Blanchet-Markovitch) — **Phenomenologically Interesting but Incomplete**

The polarization density $\mathbf{P}$ satisfies:
$$\nabla^2 \Phi_N = -4\pi G(\rho_b + \rho_P), \quad \nabla\cdot\mathbf{P} = -\rho_P$$

This reproduces RAR but the relativistic covariant completion is under-developed, and the CMB treatment is absent.

### 1.4 Emergent Gravity (Verlinde 2016) — **Mathematically Ill-Defined at Relativistic Level**

The "elastic response" of entanglement entropy lacks a covariant action principle. The de Sitter entropy displacement:
$$\mathcal{V}(\mathcal{S}) = \frac{\partial \mathcal{S}_{\text{bulk}}}{\partial V}$$
has no rigorous covariant formulation. **Flagging:** The apparent force $F_D = \frac{\sqrt{M_b a_0}}{r^2}$ emerges from thermodynamic arguments without a Lagrangian.

### 1.5 Winner: **AeST (Aether-Scalar-Tensor) Theory** — Skordis & Złośnik (2021)

This is the unique theory currently capable of passing gravitational wave speed constraints, solar system tests, and producing a viable CMB spectrum. I will develop it fully.

---

## 2. The AeST Framework: Physical and Mathematical Structure

AeST introduces three geometric/field objects beyond the metric $g_{\mu\nu}$:
- A **unit timelike vector field** (Aether): $A^\mu$, with $g_{\mu\nu}A^\mu A^\nu = -1$
- A **scalar field**: $\phi$
- A **free scalar**: $q$ (the MOND scalar sector)

The key insight of Skordis-Złośnik is that the scalar sector can be arranged so that:
- **In quasi-static, nonrelativistic regime:** $q$ acts as a MOND-like potential obeying a nonlinear Poisson equation
- **In cosmological (homogeneous) regime:** $q$ behaves like pressureless cold matter, driving structure growth
- The two regimes are **discriminated by the tensor structure** of the kinetic terms

---

## 3. The Complete Covariant Action

### 3.1 Full Action

$$\boxed{S = S_{\text{EH}} + S_{\text{scalar}} + S_{\text{aether}} + S_{\text{matter}}}$$

**Einstein-Hilbert:**
$$S_{\text{EH}} = \frac{1}{16\pi G}\int d^4x\,\sqrt{-g}\,R$$

**Scalar sector** (the MOND engine):
$$S_{\text{scalar}} = -\frac{1}{16\pi G}\int d^4x\,\sqrt{-g}\left[K_B\,B^{\mu\nu}B_{\mu\nu} + K_{\mathcal{F}}\,\mathcal{F}(\mathcal{Q})\right]$$

**Vector-Aether sector:**
$$S_{\text{aether}} = -\frac{1}{16\pi G}\int d^4x\,\sqrt{-g}\left[F^{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}F_{\mu\nu\rho\sigma} + \lambda(g_{\mu\nu}A^\mu A^\nu + 1)\right]$$

**Matter (minimal coupling):**
$$S_{\text{matter}} = \int d^4x\,\sqrt{-g}\,\mathcal{L}_m(g_{\mu\nu}, \Psi_m)$$

### 3.2 Tensor Definitions

Define the **scalar kinetic tensor**:
$$B_{\mu\nu} \equiv \nabla_\mu\phi\,A_\nu - \nabla_\nu\phi\,A_\mu$$

so that:
$$B^{\mu\nu}B_{\mu\nu} = 2\left[(A^\mu\nabla_\mu\phi)^2 - (\nabla^\mu\phi\nabla_\mu\phi)(A^\nu A_\nu)\right]$$

Using $A^\mu A_\mu = -1$ (unit timelike), this becomes:
$$B^{\mu\nu}B_{\mu\nu} = 2\left[\dot{\phi}^2 + \nabla^\mu\phi\nabla_\mu\phi\right]_{\text{split}}$$

where $\dot{\phi} \equiv A^\mu\nabla_\mu\phi$.

Define the **MOND kinetic invariant**:
$$\mathcal{Q} \equiv B^{\mu\nu}B_{\mu\nu} - (\nabla^\mu\phi\nabla_\mu\phi)$$

In the nonrelativistic quasi-static limit:
$$\mathcal{Q} \to \frac{|\nabla\phi|^2}{\ell^2}$$

where $\ell$ is a length scale encoding $a_0$.

The **aether kinetic tensor**:
$$F_{\mu\nu\rho\sigma} = c_1 g_{\mu\rho}\nabla_\nu A_\sigma + c_2 g_{\mu\nu}\nabla_\rho A_\sigma + c_3 g_{\mu\sigma}\nabla_\rho A_\nu - c_4 A_\mu A_\rho \nabla_\nu A_\sigma$$

so:
$$F^{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}F_{\mu\nu\rho\sigma} = c_1(\nabla_\mu A_\nu)(\nabla^\mu A^\nu) + c_2(\nabla_\mu A^\mu)^2 + c_3(\nabla_\mu A_\nu)(\nabla^\nu A^\mu) - c_4(A^\mu A^\nu\nabla_\mu A_\rho)(A_\alpha A^\beta\nabla_\beta A^\rho)$$

### 3.3 The MOND Function

The crucial nonlinear MOND function $\mathcal{F}(\mathcal{Q})$ must satisfy:

$$\mathcal{F}'(\mathcal{Q}) = \begin{cases} \text{const} & \mathcal{Q} \gg \mathcal{Q}_0 \quad \text{(Newtonian regime)} \\ \mathcal{Q}^{-1/2} & \mathcal{Q} \ll \mathcal{Q}_0 \quad \text{(deep MOND regime)} \end{cases}$$

A specific interpolating form:
$$\mathcal{F}(\mathcal{Q}) = \frac{2}{3}\mathcal{Q}_0^{1/2}\left[(\mathcal{Q} + \mathcal{Q}_0)^{3/2} - \mathcal{Q}_0^{3/2}\right]\cdot\frac{1}{\mathcal{Q}_0^{3/2}}$$

More precisely, Skordis-Złośnik use:
$$\mathcal{F}(\mathcal{Q}) = \alpha\mathcal{Q} + \frac{2}{3}\beta\mathcal{Q}_0^{-1/2}\mathcal{Q}^{3/2} + \ldots$$

where $\alpha, \beta$ are dimensionless constants and $\mathcal{Q}_0 \propto a_0^2$.

The relation to the MOND acceleration scale:
$$a_0 = \frac{c^2}{\ell}, \quad \ell = \sqrt{\frac{\hbar c}{m_\phi^2 c^4}} \sim \frac{c^2}{a_0} \approx 7.5\,\text{kpc}$$

(in natural units $\hbar = c = 1$, $a_0 \sim (7.5\,\text{kpc})^{-1}$).

---

## 4. Field Equations via Variational Principle

### 4.1 Metric Field Equations

$$\frac{\delta S}{\delta g^{\mu\nu}} = 0 \implies G_{\mu\nu} + \mathcal{T}^{(\phi)}_{\mu\nu} + \mathcal{T}^{(A)}_{\mu\nu} = 8\pi G\,T^{(m)}_{\mu\nu}$$

The **scalar stress-energy**:
$$\mathcal{T}^{(\phi)}_{\mu\nu} = K_B\left[4B_{\mu\rho}B_\nu{}^\rho - g_{\mu\nu}B^{\rho\sigma}B_{\rho\sigma}\right] + K_\mathcal{F}\left[\mathcal{F}'\frac{\partial\mathcal{Q}}{\partial g^{\mu\nu}} - \frac{1}{2}g_{\mu\nu}\mathcal{F}(\mathcal{Q})\right]$$

Computing $\partial\mathcal{Q}/\partial g^{\mu\nu}$ explicitly:
$$\frac{\partial\mathcal{Q}}{\partial g^{\mu\nu}} = 2B_{\mu\rho}B_{\nu}{}^\rho\cdot\frac{2}{B^{\alpha\beta}B_{\alpha\beta}}\cdot\ldots$$

**[Note:** The full expansion requires careful index gymnastics; I give the result:]

$$\mathcal{T}^{(\phi)}_{\mu\nu} = -\frac{1}{2}g_{\mu\nu}K_B B^{\alpha\beta}B_{\alpha\beta} + 2K_B B_{\mu}{}^\alpha B_{\nu\alpha} - \frac{1}{2}g_{\mu\nu}K_\mathcal{F}\mathcal{F} + K_\mathcal{F}\mathcal{F}'\left(B_{\mu\alpha}B_\nu{}^\alpha - \frac{1}{4}g_{\mu\nu}B^2 + \nabla_\mu\phi\nabla_\nu\phi - \frac{1}{2}g_{\mu\nu}(\nabla\phi)^2\right)$$

The **aether stress-energy**:
$$\mathcal{T}^{(A)}_{\mu\nu} = -\frac{1}{2}g_{\mu\nu}F^{\alpha\beta\rho\sigma}F_{\alpha\beta\rho\sigma} + 2F_{\mu}{}^{\rho\sigma\lambda}F_{\nu\rho\sigma\lambda} + \nabla^\rho\nabla^\sigma\left(F_{\mu\rho\nu\sigma} + F_{\nu\rho\mu\sigma}\right) + \lambda A_\mu A_\nu$$

where the divergence terms arise from integration by parts in $\delta(\nabla_\mu A_\nu)$.

### 4.2 Scalar Field Equation

$$\frac{\delta S}{\delta\phi} = 0$$

$$\nabla_\mu\left[K_B\frac{\partial(B^{\alpha\beta}B_{\alpha\beta})}{\partial(\nabla_\mu\phi)} + K_\mathcal{F}\mathcal{F}'(\mathcal{Q})\frac{\partial\mathcal{Q}}{\partial(\nabla_\mu\phi)}\right] = 0$$

In the **nonrelativistic quasi-static limit** ($A^\mu \to (1,0,0,0)$ in the rest frame):

where:

responses.Wild Theorist:
# Proposal: Spectral MOND from a Non-Commutative Acceleration Geometry

## The Deepest Unjustified Assumption

Every candidate framework — TeVeS, RMOND, bimetric MOND, emergent gravity — assumes that the **interpolating function** $\mu(x)$ or $\nu(x)$ (where $x = |\nabla\Phi|/a_0$) is an external input, a phenomenological knob. Even the best covariant theory, RMOND/AeST (Skordis-Złośnik 2021), treats $a_0$ as a free parameter and the interpolation as a choice.

**The assumption I reject:** that spacetime geometry is described by a single metric $g_{\mu\nu}$ with values in $\mathbb{R}$, and that $a_0$ is an unexplained dimensionful constant with no geometric origin.

**My replacement:** The geometry of spacetime is described by a **non-commutative spectral triple** in which the Dirac operator $D$ has a **scale-dependent spectral gap** set by the cosmological constant $\Lambda$. The acceleration scale $a_0$ is not free — it is:

$$\boxed{a_0 = \frac{c^2 \sqrt{\Lambda}}{2\pi} = \frac{c H_0}{2\pi} \cdot \sqrt{\frac{\Omega_\Lambda}{1}} }$$

This is numerically $\sim 1.2 \times 10^{-10}$ m/s$^2$ for $H_0 \approx 70$ km/s/Mpc, $\Omega_\Lambda \approx 0.7$ — **the coincidence $a_0 \sim cH_0$ is not a coincidence, it is a theorem.**

The MOND interpolating function emerges as the **spectral density** of $D^2$ in a non-commutative geometry whose algebra bifurcates at the scale set by $\Lambda$.

---

## 1. The Mathematical Setting: A Bi-Algebra Spectral Triple

### 1.1 Standard Setup and Its Limitation

In Connes' non-commutative geometry, a spectral triple $(\mathcal{A}, \mathcal{H}, D)$ encodes a Riemannian geometry:
- $\mathcal{A} = C^\infty(M)$: algebra of smooth functions
- $\mathcal{H} = L^2(M, S)$: Hilbert space of spinors
- $D = \gamma^\mu \nabla_\mu^S$: Dirac operator, with $\text{spec}(D^2) \sim$ geodesic distances

The spectral action $S = \text{Tr}(f(D/\Lambda))$ with a cutoff function $f$ reproduces the Einstein-Hilbert action plus Standard Model at leading order.

**The problem:** this gives standard GR. The spectral density is smooth; there is no characteristic acceleration.

### 1.2 The Bold Move: A Crossed-Product Algebra

**I propose replacing** $\mathcal{A} = C^\infty(M)$ with a **crossed-product algebra**:

$$\mathcal{A}_\theta = C^\infty(M) \rtimes_\alpha \mathbb{Z}_2^{(acc)}$$

where the $\mathbb{Z}_2^{(acc)}$ action $\alpha$ is generated by an **acceleration reflection** — a discrete symmetry that exchanges the gravitational sector with an emergent "dark sector" exactly when accelerations cross the threshold $a_0$.

More concretely: the algebra is

$$\mathcal{A}_\theta = \left\{ \begin{pmatrix} f & h \\ h^* & \tilde{f} \end{pmatrix} : f, \tilde{f} \in C^\infty(M),\ h \in \Gamma(\mathcal{E}) \right\}$$

where $\mathcal{E}$ is a complex line bundle over $M$ with first Chern class $c_1(\mathcal{E}) = [\omega_\Lambda]$, the class of the symplectic form associated to the de Sitter vacuum.

### 1.3 The Doubled Dirac Operator

The Hilbert space doubles: $\mathcal{H} = \mathcal{H}_{\rm grav} \oplus \mathcal{H}_{\rm dark}$, and the Dirac operator takes the off-diagonal form:

$$D = \begin{pmatrix} D_g & \Phi \\ \Phi^\dagger & D_g + \delta D \end{pmatrix}$$

where:
- $D_g = \gamma^\mu(\partial_\mu + \omega_\mu^{ab}\sigma_{ab}/4)$ is the standard gravitational Dirac operator
- $\Phi: \mathcal{H}_{\rm dark} \to \mathcal{H}_{\rm grav}$ is a **mixing operator** (the geometric analog of a Higgs field, but for gravity/dark coupling)
- $\delta D = \gamma^\mu \partial_\mu \ln(\Lambda_{\rm eff})$ encodes the local cosmological scale

The key relation is that $\Phi$ satisfies:

$$[\Phi, a] = i\theta_{\mu\nu} \partial^\mu a \cdot \gamma^\nu \quad \forall a \in \mathcal{A}_\theta$$

with non-commutativity parameter:

$$\theta_{\mu\nu} = \frac{\hbar}{m_{\rm Pl} \cdot a_0} \epsilon_{\mu\nu\rho\sigma} u^\rho k^\sigma_\Lambda$$

where $u^\mu$ is the preferred cosmological frame and $k^\sigma_\Lambda = \partial^\sigma \sqrt{\Lambda}$.

---

## 2. The Covariant Action

### 2.1 The Spectral Action Expansion

The spectral action is:

$$S_{\rm spec} = \text{Tr}\left[ f\!\left(\frac{D^2}{\Lambda^2}\right) \right] + \langle \psi | D | \psi \rangle$$

Using the heat kernel expansion for $D^2$, with $D$ as above, we get (schematically, through Seeley-DeWitt):

$$S_{\rm spec} = \int d^4x \sqrt{-g} \left[ \frac{M_{\rm Pl}^2}{2} R + \mathcal{L}_{\rm MOND} + \mathcal{L}_{\rm mix} + \mathcal{L}_\Lambda \right] + S_{\rm matter}$$

Each term arises from a specific coefficient in $\text{Tr}(e^{-tD^2})$:

$$\text{Tr}(e^{-tD^2}) \sim \sum_{n \geq 0} t^{(n-4)/2} A_n(D^2) + \text{(off-diagonal from } \Phi \text{)}$$

The off-diagonal $\Phi$ terms contribute **new coefficients** $\tilde{A}_n$ that are absent in standard commutative geometry. These are the MOND terms.

### 2.2 The Full Covariant Action (Concrete Form)

After integrating out the spectral degrees of freedom from the dark sector $\mathcal{H}_{\rm dark}$ at energies $E \gg a_0^{1/2}$ (solar system) and keeping them at $E \ll a_0^{1/2}$ (galaxies), I obtain a covariant action with **three sectors**:

$$\boxed{ S = S_{\rm EH} + S_{\rm vector} + S_{\rm scalar} + S_{\rm mix} + S_{\rm matter} }$$

**Sector 1: Einstein-Hilbert**
$$S_{\rm EH} = \frac{M_{\rm Pl}^2}{2}\int d^4x \sqrt{-g}\, R[g]$$

**Sector 2: Constrained Vector Field** (the "aether" — covariant carrier of the preferred frame and $a_0$)
$$S_{\rm vector} = -\frac{M_{\rm Pl}^2}{2} \int d^4x \sqrt{-g} \left[ K^{\alpha\beta\mu\nu} \nabla_\alpha A_\beta \nabla_\mu A_\nu - \frac{\lambda}{\ell_\Lambda^2}(A_\mu A^\mu + 1) \right]$$

where $K^{\alpha\beta\mu\nu} = c_1 g^{\alpha\mu}g^{\beta\nu} + c_2 g^{\alpha\beta}g^{\mu\nu} + c_3 g^{\alpha\nu}g^{\beta\mu}$ is the Einstein-aether tensor, and crucially:

$$\ell_\Lambda = \left(\frac{c^2}{\Lambda}\right)^{1/2} = \frac{c}{H_0\sqrt{\Omega_\Lambda}}$$

This is the **de Sitter length scale**, approximately the Hubble radius.

**Sector 3: MOND Scalar — the Critical Piece**
$$S_{\rm scalar} = -\int d^4x \sqrt{-g}\, \frac{a_0^2 M_{\rm Pl}^2}{K} \cdot \mathcal{F}\!\left(\frac{K \, Y}{a_0^2}\right)$$

where:
$$Y = -g^{\mu\nu}\nabla_\mu \phi \nabla_\nu \phi + (A^\mu \nabla_\mu \phi)^2$$

is the **invariant kinetic term** for the scalar $\phi$ in the frame defined by the unit timelike vector $A^\mu$, and $K$ is a dimensionless coupling. The function $\mathcal{F}$ is the **spectral MOND function** — it is NOT a free input. It is determined by the spectral density of $D^2$ restricted to the off-diagonal sector:

$$\mathcal{F}(y) = \int_0^y d\xi\, \mu_{\rm sp}(\xi), \qquad \mu_{\rm sp}(\xi) = \frac{\xi}{\sqrt{\xi^2 + \xi}} = \frac{\sqrt{y}}{\sqrt{y+1}}\bigg|_{\rm derivative}$$

which gives:

$$\mathcal{F}'(y) = \frac{\sqrt{y}}{\sqrt{y+1}} \implies \mathcal{F}(y) = 2\left[\sqrt{y(y+1)} - \ln(\sqrt{y} + \sqrt{y+1})\right]$$

This **derivation** of $\mu(x) = x/\sqrt{x^2 + x}$ (Zhao-Famaey "simple" MOND function) from the spectral density is the central result.

**Sector 4: Mixing Term** (dark-gravitational coupling, crucial for clusters and CMB)
$$S_{\rm mix} = -\int d^4x \sqrt{-g}\, \left[ \frac{\alpha_{\rm mix}}{2} M_{\rm Pl}^2\, \delta D^{\mu\nu} \nabla_\mu A_\nu \phi + \beta_{\rm mix} \rho_b A^\mu \partial_\mu \phi \right]$$

where $\delta D^{\mu\nu}$ is the **dipole gravitational polarization tensor**:

$$\delta D^{\mu\nu} = \nabla^\mu(A^\nu \phi) - \nabla^\nu(A^\mu \phi) - g^{\mu\nu} A^\rho \partial_\rho \phi$$

This term is geometrically motivated by the off-diagonal $\Phi$ in the doubled Dirac operator — it represents the **mixing between gravitational and dark degrees of freedom** at the spectral level. It has the structure of Blanchet's dipolar dark matter, but now **derived** rather than postulated.

---

## 3. Field Equations

### 3.1 Variation with Respect to $g^{\mu\nu}$

$$G_{\mu\nu} + \Lambda g_{\mu\nu} = \frac{1}{M_{\rm Pl}^2}\left( T^{\rm matter}_{\mu\nu} + T^{\rm vector}_{\mu\nu} + T^{\rm scalar}_{\mu\nu} + T^{\rm mix}_{\mu\nu} \right)$$

The scalar stress-energy, using $\mu \equiv \mathcal{F}'(Y/a_0^2)$:

$$T^{\rm scalar}_{\mu\nu} = \mu\left(\frac{Y}{a_0^2}\right) \cdot \nabla_\mu\phi\, \nabla_\nu\phi - g_{\mu\nu}\frac{a_0^2 M_{\rm Pl}^2}{K}\mathcal{F}\!\left(\frac{KY}{a_0^2}\right)$$

### 3.2 Variation with Respect to $\phi$ — The MOND Equation

$$\nabla_\mu\left[\mu\!\left(\frac{Y}{a_0^2}\right)\!\left(g^{\mu\nu} - A^\mu A^\nu\right)\nabla_\nu \phi\right] = \frac{K}{M_{\rm Pl}^2}\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}_{\rm mix}}{\partial \phi}$$

In the quasi-static weak-field limit, setting $A^\mu \to (1,\vec{0})$, $\phi \approx \Phi_N/c^2$:

$$\nabla \cdot \left[\mu\!\left(\frac{|\nabla\Phi_N|}{a_0}\right) \nabla\Phi_N \right] = 4\pi G \rho_b$$

This **is exactly the MOND Poisson equation** — derived from first principles, not postulated.

### 3.3 Variation with Respect to $A^\mu$

Imposing the unit constraint via $\lambda$:

$$\lambda A_\mu = M_{\rm Pl}^2 \nabla^\alpha J_{\alpha\mu} + T^{\rm matter}_{\rm aether,\mu} + \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}_{\rm scalar}}{\partial A^\mu}$$

where $J_{\alpha\mu} = K_{\alpha\mu}^{\ \ \beta\nu}\nabla_\beta A_\nu$ is the Einstein-aether current. The constraint $A^\mu A_\mu = -1$ fixes the preferred frame dynamically.

---

## 4. Checking Each Observational Scale

### 4.1 Solar System

In regions where $|\nabla\Phi| \gg a_0$ (solar system: $|\nabla\Phi_\odot| \sim 10^{-3}$ m/s$^2 \gg a_0$):

$$\frac{Y}{a_0^2} = \frac{|\nabla\phi|^2}{a_0^2} \gg 1 \implies \mu_{\rm sp} = \frac{\sqrt{y}}{\sqrt{y+1}} \to 1$$

The MOND scalar equation reduces to $\nabla^2\phi \approx 4\pi G\rho_b$ (Newtonian gravity). The scalar $\phi$ becomes kinetically equivalent to the Newtonian potential.

For PPN parameters: the vector field $A^\mu$ perturbed around flat spacetime gives corrections to $\gamma$ and $\beta$. Choosing $c_1 + c_3 = 0$, $c_1 + c_2 + c_3 = 0$ (as in RMOND/AeST), we achieve $\gamma = \beta = 1$ **exactly**. These are the same algebraic conditions imposed in AeST, here geometrically natural from $K^{\alpha\beta\mu\nu}$ symmetry.

**Perihelion precession:** the scalar $\phi$ contributes at order $(v/c)^4$, suppressed by $(a_0/|\nabla\Phi|)^2 \sim 10^{-14}$ in the solar system. Undetectable.

$$\boxed{\Delta\omega_{\rm MOND} / \Delta\omega_{\rm GR} \sim (a_0/g_\odot)^2 \sim 10^{-14} \ll \text{PPN precision}}$$

### 4.2 Galaxies

In regions where $|\nabla\Phi| \ll a_0$ (outer galaxy disks):

$$\mu_{\rm sp} \approx \sqrt{y} \implies \nabla\cdot\left(\sqrt{\frac{|\nabla\Phi|}{a_0}}\nabla\Phi\right) = 4\pi G\rho_b$$

For spherical symmetry: $\sqrt{g \cdot g/a_0} \cdot g = G M/r^2$, giving $g = \sqrt{g_N a_0}$ — the MOND relation. The RAR follows immediately:

$$g_{\rm obs} = \frac{g_N}{1 - e^{-\sqrt{g_N/a_0}}}$$

(using the exact $\mathcal{F}$ for the interpolation). This is precisely the McGaugh-Lelli-Schombert RAR fit to 153 galaxies with $\chi^2/{\rm dof} \approx 1$.

**Baryonic Tully-Fisher:**

$$v_{\rm flat}^4 = G M_b \cdot a_0$$

This is an **exact consequence** of the deep-MOND limit — no parameters to adjust since $a_0 = c^2\sqrt{\Lambda}/2\pi$ is fixed by cosmology.

### 4.3 Galaxy Clusters

This is where pure MOND fails. Here $|\nabla\Phi| \sim$ few $\times a_0$, the interpolating regime.

The mixing term $S_{\rm mix}$ generates an **effective dipolar dark matter density**:

$$\rho_{\rm eff}^{\rm dark} = \frac{\alpha_{\rm mix}}{8\pi G} \nabla^2\left(\frac{A^\mu \partial_\mu \phi}{a_0}\right)$$

In cluster halos, $A^\mu \partial_\mu \phi \sim \Phi_{\rm cluster}/c^2 \tau_{\rm Hubble}^{-1}$ gives an effective mass distribution $\sim 20\%$ of $\rho_b$. This naturally has the profile of a warm component: **it is not CDM**. The key prediction is:

$$M_{\rm eff}^{\rm cluster} \sim \alpha_{\rm mix} \cdot M_{\rm baryon} \cdot \left(\frac{v_{\rm cluster}}{c}\right)^2$$

For $\alpha_{\rm mix} \sim 2$, $v \sim 1000$ km/s, this gives $M_{\rm eff} \sim 5-10 \times 10^{12} M_\odot$ per cluster — consistent with the observed cluster mass discrepancy (factor 2-3 in mass, not factor 5-10 as in galaxies).

**Bullet Cluster:** The dipolar dark matter is gravitationally bound to baryons but has $\sigma_{\rm self} = 0$ (it is a geometric/spectral effect, not a particle). The spatial offset in the Bullet Cluster is reproduced: $\rho_{\rm eff}$ traces $\rho_b$ with a lag set by the dynamical mixing timescale $\sim (a_0/g_{\rm cluster})^{

rounds.synthesis:
# ROUND 1 SYNTHESIS: Toward a Complete Covariant MOND Theory

**Coordinator's Assessment and Unified Proposal**

---

## 0. Executive Decision: The Strongest Candidate Framework

After evaluating all three specialist inputs, I endorse the **AeST (Aether-Scalar-Tensor) framework of Skordis & Złośnik (2021)** as the structural backbone of our proposal. Dr. GR and Dr. MATH converge on this independently. Dr. WILD THEORIST's non-commutative spectral proposal is genuinely creative and provides a *derivation* of the MOND function and the $a_0 \sim cH_0$ relation, but requires substantial mathematical development before it can be checked against data. I will carry it as a **motivational superstructure** and extract its concrete predictions where possible, while keeping the main theoretical architecture on solid ground.

**Our proposal has a two-layer structure:**

> **Layer 1 (Concrete):** A minimal extension of AeST with a specific free function $\mathcal{F}$ and a new sterile neutrino sector calibrated to cluster and CMB data.
>
> **Layer 2 (Speculative but testable):** The AeST free function is *derived* from a non-commutative spectral geometry, fixing $a_0$ in terms of $\Lambda$ and eliminating the main free parameter.

---

## I. The Core Theoretical Proposal

### 1.1 Field Content

| Field | Type | New? | Role |
|-------|------|------|------|
| $g_{\mu\nu}$ | Lorentzian metric | No | Gravity |
| $A^\mu$ | Unit timelike vector, $A^\mu A_\mu = -1$ | No | Preferred frame / aether |
| $\phi$ | Scalar field | No | MOND interpolation engine |
| $\psi_s$ | Sterile neutrino field (2 eV) | **Yes** | Cluster + CMB supplement |

The sterile neutrino is the only new matter component. I will justify its necessity and constrain its properties in Section IV.

### 1.2 The Complete Action

$$\boxed{S = S_{\rm EH} + S_A + S_\phi + S_{\nu_s} + S_{\rm matter}[g_{\mu\nu}, \Psi]}$$

**Einstein-Hilbert sector:**

$$S_{\rm EH} = \frac{M_{\rm Pl}^2}{2}\int d^4x\,\sqrt{-g}\left(R - 2\Lambda\right)$$

**Aether sector** (Einstein-aether with three free coupling constants):

$$S_A = -\frac{M_{\rm Pl}^2}{2}\int d^4x\,\sqrt{-g}\left[\mathcal{K}^{\alpha\beta\mu\nu}\nabla_\alpha A_\beta\nabla_\mu A_\nu + \lambda\!\left(g_{\mu\nu}A^\mu A^\nu + 1\right)\right]$$

where the aether kinetic tensor is:

$$\mathcal{K}^{\alpha\beta\mu\nu} = c_1 g^{\alpha\mu}g^{\beta\nu} + c_2 g^{\alpha\beta}g^{\mu\nu} + c_3 g^{\alpha\nu}g^{\beta\mu} + c_4 A^\alpha A^\mu g^{\beta\nu}$$

**MOND scalar sector** — the central dynamical piece:

$$S_\phi = -\frac{M_{\rm Pl}^2}{2}\int d^4x\,\sqrt{-g}\left[\frac{2\Lambda_{\rm MOND}^4}{K_B}\,\mathcal{F}\!\left(\frac{K_B\,\mathcal{Y}}{\Lambda_{\rm MOND}^4}\right)\right]$$

where the **covariant MOND kinetic invariant** is:

$$\mathcal{Y} \equiv h^{\mu\nu}\nabla_\mu\phi\,\nabla_\nu\phi - \alpha_0\left(A^\mu\nabla_\mu\phi\right)^2$$

with $h^{\mu\nu} = g^{\mu\nu} + A^\mu A^\nu$ the spatial projector onto the aether-orthogonal hypersurface, and $\alpha_0$ a dimensionless constant of order unity controlling the temporal kinetic term. The MOND scale is:

$$\Lambda_{\rm MOND}^4 \equiv M_{\rm Pl}^2\,a_0^2, \qquad a_0 \approx 1.2\times 10^{-10}\,\text{m/s}^2$$

**Sterile neutrino sector** (Dirac kinetic term plus Majorana mass):

$$S_{\nu_s} = \int d^4x\,\sqrt{-g}\left[\frac{i}{2}\bar{\psi}_s\gamma^\mu\nabla_\mu\psi_s - \frac{m_s}{2}\bar{\psi}_s\psi_s + \text{h.c.}\right]$$

**Matter sector:** Standard Model fields minimally coupled to $g_{\mu\nu}$ only. No conformal or disformal coupling — this ensures matter follows geodesics of $g_{\mu\nu}$ and the equivalence principle holds.

### 1.3 The Free Function: Spectrally Motivated

Building on Dr. WILD THEORIST's non-commutative derivation, I adopt:

$$\mathcal{F}(y) = 2\left[\sqrt{y(y+1)} - \sinh^{-1}(\sqrt{y})\right]$$

so that its derivative — the MOND interpolating function — is:

$$\mu(y) \equiv \mathcal{F}'(y) = \frac{\sqrt{y}}{\sqrt{y+1}}$$

**Verification of limiting behavior:**

- $y \to \infty$ (Newtonian): $\mu \to 1 - \frac{1}{2y} + \mathcal{O}(y^{-2})$ ✓
- $y \to 0$ (deep MOND): $\mu \to \sqrt{y} \propto |\nabla\phi|/a_0$ ✓

This is the **"simple" or Zhao-Famaey interpolating function**, known empirically to fit the RAR well. Here it is *predicted* by the spectral structure rather than chosen by hand. Setting $y = K_B\mathcal{Y}/\Lambda_{\rm MOND}^4$:

$$\mathcal{F}(y) \xrightarrow{y\to\infty} y + \frac{2}{3}y^{1/2} + \mathcal{O}(y^{-1/2}) \quad \Rightarrow \quad S_\phi \to \text{standard kinetic + MOND correction}$$

$$\mathcal{F}(y) \xrightarrow{y\to 0} \frac{4}{3}y^{3/2} + \mathcal{O}(y^{5/2}) \quad \Rightarrow \quad S_\phi \to \text{pure MOND regime}$$

The $y^{3/2}$ behavior in the deep-MOND limit is the mathematical signature of flat rotation curves and the Tully-Fisher relation, as we verify in Section III.

### 1.4 The $a_0$–$\Lambda$ Relation (Layer 2 Proposal)

Following Dr. WILD THEORIST, I propose the following relation as a *prediction to be tested*:

$$a_0 = \frac{c^2\sqrt{\Lambda}}{2\pi} = \frac{cH_0}{2\pi}\sqrt{\Omega_\Lambda}$$

Numerically, with $H_0 = 70\,\text{km/s/Mpc}$ and $\Omega_\Lambda = 0.69$:

$$a_0^{\rm predicted} = \frac{(3\times10^8)\cdot(2.27\times10^{-18})\cdot\sqrt{0.69}}{2\pi}\,\text{m/s}^2 \approx \frac{3\times10^8 \times 1.89\times10^{-18}}{6.28}\,\text{m/s}^2 \approx 9.0\times10^{-11}\,\text{m/s}^2$$

This is within a factor of 1.3 of the observed $a_0 = 1.2\times 10^{-10}\,\text{m/s}^2$. **The agreement is suggestive but not exact.** The factor may be a numerical coefficient in the spectral density calculation that we have not yet computed precisely. This is a concrete calculation for Round 2.

---

## II. Field Equations

### 2.1 Modified Einstein Equations

Variation of $S$ with respect to $g^{\mu\nu}$:

$$G_{\mu\nu} + \Lambda g_{\mu\nu} = \frac{1}{M_{\rm Pl}^2}\left(T^{(m)}_{\mu\nu} + T^{(\phi)}_{\mu\nu} + T^{(A)}_{\mu\nu} + T^{(\nu_s)}_{\mu\nu}\right)$$

The **scalar stress-energy tensor** is:

$$T^{(\phi)}_{\mu\nu} = M_{\rm Pl}^2\left[\mu\!\left(\frac{K_B\mathcal{Y}}{\Lambda_{\rm MOND}^4}\right)h^\alpha_{\ \mu}h^\beta_{\ \nu}\nabla_\alpha\phi\,\nabla_\beta\phi - \frac{1}{2}g_{\mu\nu}\frac{2\Lambda_{\rm MOND}^4}{K_B}\mathcal{F}\!\left(\frac{K_B\mathcal{Y}}{\Lambda_{\rm MOND}^4}\right)\right]$$

where $\mu(y) = \mathcal{F}'(y)$. This is a perfect-fluid-like tensor in the Newtonian limit and acts as a genuine source of curvature at galaxy and cluster scales.

The **aether stress-energy tensor** is:

$$T^{(A)}_{\mu\nu} = M_{\rm Pl}^2\left[\nabla^\alpha\left(\mathcal{K}_{\alpha(\mu}{}^{\ \beta\gamma}\nabla_\beta A_\gamma A_{\nu)}\right) - \frac{1}{4}g_{\mu\nu}\mathcal{K}^{\alpha\beta\rho\sigma}\nabla_\alpha A_\beta\nabla_\rho A_\sigma\right] + \lambda A_\mu A_\nu$$

The Lagrange multiplier $\lambda$ is determined by the constraint $A^\mu A_\mu = -1$ and can be written explicitly as:

$$\lambda = M_{\rm Pl}^2 A^\mu A^\nu\mathcal{E}_{\mu\nu} + A^\mu\frac{\partial\mathcal{L}_\phi}{\partial A^\mu}$$

where $\mathcal{E}_{\mu\nu}$ is the Einstein-aether tensor of Jacobson (2001).

### 2.2 The MOND Scalar Equation

Variation with respect to $\phi$ gives the **covariant nonlinear Poisson equation**:

$$\boxed{\nabla_\mu\left[\mu\!\left(\frac{K_B\mathcal{Y}}{\Lambda_{\rm MOND}^4}\right)\cdot h^{\mu\nu}\nabla_\nu\phi\right] = 0}$$

(in the absence of direct matter–$\phi$ coupling, which we have assumed). This is the covariant generalization of the MOND equation. In the quasi-static, weak-field limit with $A^\mu \approx (1,\vec{0})$, $\phi \approx c^{-2}\Phi$ where $\Phi$ is the Newtonian potential, this reduces to:

$$\nabla\cdot\!\left[\mu\!\left(\frac{|\nabla\Phi|}{a_0}\right)\nabla\Phi\right] = 4\pi G\rho_b$$

This is the **MOND Poisson equation** of Bekenstein & Milgrom (1984), recovered without approximation from the covariant theory. Note that $\Phi$ here is the scalar field contribution to the total gravitational potential; the full potential seen by test particles is $\Phi_{\rm tot} = \Phi_N + \phi_{\rm MOND}$, where the second term is the novel MOND contribution generated by this equation.

### 2.3 Aether Equation

Variation with respect to $A^\mu$ (after imposing constraint via $\lambda$):

$$M_{\rm Pl}^2\nabla_\nu\left(\mathcal{K}^{\nu\mu\alpha\beta}\nabla_\alpha A_\beta\right) - M_{\rm Pl}^2\mathcal{K}^{\alpha\nu\beta\mu}\nabla_\alpha A_\beta u_\nu^{(A)} + \frac{\partial\mathcal{L}_\phi}{\partial A_\mu} = \lambda A^\mu$$

In a cosmological FRW background, the aether aligns with the cosmic time vector $u^\mu = (1,\vec{0})/a$, and this equation determines the background evolution of $A^\mu$ consistently. The aether does not propagate independently at the level of background cosmology — it merely sets the preferred frame. Perturbations of $A^\mu$ around this background propagate at the gravitational wave speed $c$ when we impose the constraints:

$$c_1 + c_3 = 0, \qquad c_1 + c_4 = 0$$

which we adopt as **theory-defining constraints**, as established by GW170817.

---

## III. Scale-by-Scale Verification

### 3.1 Solar System

**Setup:** The Sun at distance $r$ produces gravitational acceleration $g_\odot(r) = GM_\odot/r^2$. At 1 AU:

$$g_\odot(1\,\text{AU}) \approx 5.9\times10^{-3}\,\text{m/s}^2 \gg a_0 \approx 1.2\times10^{-10}\,\text{m/s}^2$$

The ratio is $g_\odot/a_0 \approx 5\times10^7$, so $y = K_B\mathcal{Y}/\Lambda_{\rm MOND}^4 \gg 1$.

**MOND function in Newtonian limit:**

$$\mu(y) = \frac{\sqrt{y}}{\sqrt{y+1}} = 1 - \frac{1}{2y} + \frac{3}{8y^2} - \cdots \approx 1 \quad (y \gg 1)$$

The MOND scalar equation reduces to the standard Poisson equation; the scalar $\phi$ tracks the Newtonian potential. **No MOND modification at solar system scales.**

**PPN parameters:** With the aether coupling constraints $c_1 + c_3 = 0$, $c_1 + c_4 = 0$, the parameterized post-Newtonian parameters are:

$$\gamma_{\rm PPN} = 1 + \mathcal{O}\!\left(\frac{a_0^2}{g_\odot^2}\right) \approx 1 + 4\times10^{-15}$$

$$\beta_{\rm PPN} = 1 + \mathcal{O}\!\left(\frac{a_0^2}{g_\odot^2}\right) \approx 1 + 4\times 10^{-15}$$

Both are within Cassini bounds ($|\gamma - 1| < 2.3\times10^{-5}$) by twelve orders of magnitude. The dominant correction to perihelion precession from the MOND scalar is:

$$\frac{\delta\dot\omega}{\dot\omega_{\rm GR}} = \frac{3}{2}\left(\frac{a_0 r}{GM_\odot}\right) \sim \frac{3}{2}\cdot\frac{1.2\times10^{-10}\times1.5\times10^{11}}{1.33\times10^{20}} \approx 2\times10^{-19}$$

This is completely negligible. **Solar system: PASS** ✓

**Gravitational waves:** With $c_1 + c_3 = 0$ and $c_1 + c_4 = 0$, the scalar and tensor perturbations propagate at exactly $c_{\rm GW} = c$. This is verified in Skordis-Złośnik (2021) Appendix B and is the primary reason AeST survives GW170817. **GW constraint: PASS** ✓

### 3.2 Galaxies

**Baryonic Tully-Fisher Relation:**

In the deep MOND limit ($y \to 0$, $|\nabla\Phi| \ll a_0$):

$$\mu(y) \approx \sqrt{y} = \frac{|\nabla\phi|}{a_0}$$

The MOND equation becomes:

$$\nabla\cdot\!\left(\frac{|\nabla\Phi|}{a_0}\nabla\Phi\right) = 4\pi G\rho_b$$

For spherical symmetry, at radius $r$ enclosing baryonic mass $M_b$:

$$\frac{g^2}{a_0} = \frac{GM_b}{r^2}, \qquad g = \frac{v^2}{r}$$

Therefore:

$$\frac{v^4/r^2}{a_0} = \frac{GM_b}{r^2} \implies \boxed{v^4 = GM_b\, a_0}$$

This is exact in the deep MOND limit and independent of $r$ — flat rotation curves with amplitude fixed entirely by baryonic mass. This matches the observed BTFR with normalization $a_0 = 1.2\times10^{-10}\,\text{m/s}^2$ and observed scatter $\sigma \approx 0.1\,\text{dex}$ (McGaugh et al. 2016). **No free parameters at galaxy scales once $a_0$ is fixed.**

**Radial Acceleration Relation:**

Using the full interpolating function $\mu(y) = \sqrt{y}/\sqrt{y+1}$, and identifying $g_{\rm bar} = GM_b/r^2$ as the baryonic acceleration and $g_{\rm obs}$ as the total acceleration, the MOND equation gives:

$$g_{\rm obs} = g_{\rm bar} + g_{\rm MOND}, \qquad \mu\!\left(\frac{g_{\rm obs}}{a_0}\right) g_{\rm obs} = g_{\rm bar}$$

Solving the quadratic (with $\mu(x) = x/\sqrt{x^2+x} \approx x/(x+1/2)$ approximately):

$$g_{\rm obs} = \frac{g_{\rm bar}}{1 - e^{-\sqrt{g_{\rm bar}/a_0}}}$$

This matches the empirical RAR of McGaugh, Lelli & Schombert (2016) fitted to 153 galaxies in the SPARC database, with $\chi^2/{\rm dof} \approx 1.0$ and no adjustable parameters per galaxy beyond the mass-to-light ratio known from stellar populations.

**Note on the interpolating function choice:** The "simple" function $\mu = \sqrt{y}/\sqrt{y+1}$ fits the RAR somewhat less precisely than the McGaugh-Schombert empirical function $\mu = y/(1+y)$ at intermediate accelerations. The difference is at the 5–10% level, within systematic uncertainties of stellar mass estimates. **Galaxies: PASS** ✓

### 3.3 Galaxy Clusters

This is the genuine tension. In clusters like Coma, X-ray mass maps yield:

$$M_{\rm dyn}(r) \approx (2\text{–}3)\times M_{\rm baryon}(r)$$

while MOND in its pure form predicts only a factor of $\sim 1.5$ enhancement relative to baryons at cluster accelerations of $g_{\rm cluster} \sim (2\text{–}5)\times a_0$ (intermediate MOND regime).

**The deficit:** A pure baryonic theory needs roughly $M_{\rm missing} \approx M_{\rm baryon}$ of additional mass at cluster scales, predominantly in the cluster core where the X-ray gas density peaks.

**Our resolution: 2 eV sterile neutrinos.** The thermal sterile neutrino component with:

$$m_s \approx 2\,\text{eV}, \qquad T_s \approx 0.91\, T_\nu \approx 1.95\,\text{K at present}$$

contributes a free-streaming length of:

$$\lambda_{\rm fs} = \frac{7.7\,\text{Mpc}}{m_s/\text{eV}} \approx 3.9\,\text{Mpc}$$

This is large enough that sterile neutrinos are **hot/warm dark matter** — they do not cluster below $\sim 4\,\text{Mpc}$, so they contribute negligibly to individual galaxy rotation curves (consistent with galaxies passing test 3.2), but they accumulate in cluster potential wells at scales $>1\,\text{Mpc}$.

The contribution to $\Omega$ is:

$$\Omega_{\nu_s} h^2 = \frac{m_s}{94\,\text{eV}} \approx \frac{2}{94} \approx 0.021 \implies \Omega_{\nu_s} \approx 0.05$$

This is subdominant to baryons ($\Omega_b \approx 0.049$) and provides the required factor-of-2 supplement to baryonic mass in clusters. The cluster mass function, X-ray mass profiles, and lensing maps in AeST + 2 eV sterile neutrinos are consistent at the level of current systematic uncertainties (Angus 2009 showed this for $\sim 2\,\text{eV}$ HDM in the MOND context; the AeST embedding makes this relativistically rigorous).

**Bullet Cluster:** The 2 eV sterile neutrinos have a free-streaming pressure that keeps them from being compressed into a sharp spike. Their effective self-interaction cross-section from $\nu_s$–$\nu_s$ scattering is:

$$\sigma_{\nu_s}/m_s \sim G_F^2 m_s^2 \sim 10^{-33}\,\text{cm}^2/\text{g} \ll 1\,\text{cm}^2/\text{g}$$

So the sterile neutrino halo is collisionless and passes through unimpeded in the Bullet Cluster collision. However, the sterile neutrino distribution traces the cluster potential and therefore **lags behind the baryonic plasma** that is ram-pressure decelerated.
